L1.area: Markdown rendering and content preview layer

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where rendered HTML content from a markup parser is directly injected into the DOM without sanitization, allowing attacker-controlled markup to execute arbitrary scripts when previewed.

L3.area: Markdown preview rendering and HTML output handling
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where the markdown rendering engine produces HTML that is directly assigned to the DOM via innerHTML without sanitization. Crafted markdown content containing malicious HTML tags with event handlers can execute arbitrary JavaScript when the content is previewed.

ground_truth.reason: The renderContent method renders user-supplied markdown to HTML and directly assigns the unsanitized result to innerHTML, allowing XSS via crafted HTML elements embedded in markdown files.
